description_long: This cash register features a large, boxy structure with a prominent, angled display screen set into a thick, rectangular housing. The front panel includes a wide keypad with numerous square and rectangular buttons, many of which are color-coded in blue, red, and white, and clearly labeled for various functions. To the left of the keypad is a dark, recessed area that appears to be a touchpad or possibly a receipt printer slot, with a set of vertical ridges suggesting a paper feed or card swipe. The overall body is constructed from a matte gray material, with a slightly darker shade used for the display housing. The rear and sides are plain and unadorned, emphasizing the utilitarian design. The object is robust and likely intended for use in retail or service environments, with a focus on durability and ease of use.
description: A robust, gray cash register with a large angled display, a wide multi-colored keypad, and a recessed area for receipts or cards. Its design is utilitarian and built for heavy use in retail settings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the large black display, full keypad with colored buttons, and a recessed area on the left.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the depth of the display housing and the side profile of the keypad and recessed area.
description_view_2: Rear view showing a plain, solid back with no visible features or openings.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the side of the display housing and the angled keypad, with the recessed area visible on the far side.
attribute_keypad_layout: Rectangular grid with approximately 40 keys, including numeric, function, and colored labeled buttons.
